chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْمُحْرِمِ يُضْطَرُّ إِلَى مَا لَا يَجُوزُ لَهُ لُبْسُهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 86 – The one in Ihraam is desperate to what is not allowed for him to wear

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل كالموثق أو كالحسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/297)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل كالموثق أو كالحسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/297)
text: الْمُحْرِمُ يَلْبَسُ السَّرَاوِيلَ إِذَا لَمْ يَكُنْ مَعَهُ إِزَارٌ وَ يَلْبَسُ الْخُفَّيْنِ إِذَا لَمْ يَكُنْ مَعَهُ نَعْلٌ .

en.hubeali: Humeyd Bin Ziyad, from Al Hassan Bin Muhammad Bin Sama’at, from someone else, from Aban, from Abdul Rahman, from Humran,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’farasws having said: ‘The one in Ihraam can wear the trouser when there does not happen to be a loin cloth with him; and he can wear the two shoes when there does not happen to be any slippers with him’.
en.sarwar: 6. Humayd ibn Ziyad has narrated from al-Hassan ibn Muhammad ibn Sama‘ah from more than one person from ‘Abd al-Rahman from Humran who has said the following: “Abu Ja’far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘One in the state of Ihram can wear trousers if one does not find loincloth and al-Khuffayn (shoes) if one cannot find Na’l (sandals). ‘“(Fatwa best explains this Hadith)
